tree.setup: A Sichuan noodle stall in a food court. The cook, Xiao Liu, has forearms like a sailor and a gentle voice. Everything on the menu has a small chili pepper icon next to it. The woman at the next table is fanning her mouth. The air itself seems to tingle.
tree.setup_zh: 美食广场里的一家四川面摊。厨师小刘手臂粗壮，声音却很温柔。菜单上每道菜旁边都有一个小辣椒图标。旁边桌的女人正在扇嘴巴。连空气都是辣的。

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_pinyin: nǐ hǎo! xiǎng chī miàn ma?

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Order noodles but ask for no spice.

options.text_pinyin: wǒ yào yì wǎn miàn, bú yào là de.
options.text_en: I'd like a bowl of noodles, not spicy.
options.feedback: Xiao Liu blinks twice, as if you've asked for the ocean without water. But he nods respectfully. 不要 + [thing] is the cleanest way to say 'without' at this level.
You'll get noodles -- but at a Sichuan stall, that means spicy by default. You forgot to mention 不要辣的! Xiao Liu is already reaching for the chili oil.
Xiao Liu's face lights up like a sunrise. He was born for this question. 好吃 (hǎochī, 'delicious') is one of the most useful food words you'll learn.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 好的，不辣的。你要大碗还是小碗？
turns.text_pinyin: hǎo de, bú là de. nǐ yào dà wǎn háishì xiǎo wǎn?
turns.text_en: OK, not spicy. Big bowl or small bowl?

options.feedback: Xiao Liu nods. His small bowls are what most restaurants would call medium, but he has his own system and no one questions it.
Xiao Liu grins. He loads the bowl with the quiet pride of an artist framing a canvas.
A practical question. Xiao Liu points at the price board -- small is 12, big is 15. 多少钱 will serve you well in every shop in China.

tree.cultural_note: In Sichuan-style restaurants, spicy (辣) is the default. If you can't handle heat, always specify 不辣 (bú là) or 微辣 (wēi là, 'mildly spicy'). Nobody will judge you -- much.
